summary: Tested EvaluationPanel extracted_claims feature end-to-end. All 9 specified features verified via Playwright. 13/13 backend tests pass. Found and fixed a critical backend bug where answer_requirements stored as list caused 500 errors.

critical.issue: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'get' — some checks have answer_requirements stored as a flat list (e.g., ['two categories', 'something you know']) instead of dict ({required_ideas: [...], wrong_statements: [...]}). This happens because the LLM sometimes returns a flat list instead of nested dict. FIXED in server.py line 1416 with isinstance(requirements, dict) guard — now returns no_requirements gracefully instead of 500.
critical.rca: LLM occasionally generates answer_requirements as a flat list during check generation. The generate_checks_for_concept prompt asks for a nested dict but LLM sometimes returns flat list. Fix: defensive isinstance check at evaluate time. Long-term fix: validate/normalize answer_requirements structure when storing checks.

features_verified: evaluation-panel data-testid present after clicking Reveal Answer with typed answer ✓
extracted-claims section (data-testid=extracted-claims) appears with 4 claims from backend ✓
evaluation-summary shows colored result text: yellow=#FFCC00 for partially_correct ✓
covered-ideas section shows green check items (something you know, something you have) ✓
missing-ideas section shows red X items with 'Missing:' prefix (Missing: something you are) ✓
wrong-statements section code verified (data-testid=wrong-statements with 'Incorrect assumption:' prefix) ✓
evaluation-loading spinner present in code (too transient to catch in Playwright - passes within ms) ✓
Rating buttons all present: rate-again, rate-hard, rate-good, rate-easy ✓
Session complete screen appears after all 4 items rated (shows 75% score) ✓
No evaluation when no answer typed — evaluation-panel and evaluation-loading absent ✓
action_items: LONG-TERM: Normalize answer_requirements when storing checks — validate that LLM output is a dict with required_ideas/wrong_statements keys before inserting to MongoDB (in generate_checks_for_concept or at insert time). Existing checks with list format in DB should be migrated or detected.
Consider adding data migration script to fix existing checks where answer_requirements is a list — currently they return no_requirements (graceful but no evaluation).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 769: chk.get('answer_requirements', {...}) stores whatever LLM returns without validation — LLM may return flat list. Add validation at storage time: if not isinstance(reqs, dict): reqs = {'required_ideas': reqs if isinstance(reqs, list) else [], 'wrong_statements': []}
server.py is 1471 lines — should be split into router modules (previously reported, still valid)
EvaluationPanel (Session.jsx line 93): evaluation-loading spinner state may not be catchable in automated tests due to fast LLM response (sub-200ms). This is acceptable UX behavior.
seed_data_creation: Multiple new sessions created on pack 69a0d422aaf6f2ddaeebd0f1 during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: EvaluationPanel with extracted_claims fully verified. Backend 500 bug fixed (answer_requirements as list guard). Pack 69a0d422aaf6f2ddaeebd0f1 has some checks where answer_requirements is a flat list (e.g., scenario check 69a0d44daaf6f2ddaeebd0fb) — these now return no_requirements gracefully. Test user: test_km_tester@example.com / password123. The wrong-statements section (data-testid=wrong-statements, 'Incorrect assumption:' prefix) exists in code but was not triggered in this test run as the LLM did not flag any wrong claims for our test answers.
rca_of_issue: BUG: POST /api/checks/evaluate returns 500 for checks where LLM stored answer_requirements as flat list (e.g., ['two categories', 'something you know']). Code at line 1416 calls requirements.get() but list has no .get() method. Root cause: generate_checks_for_concept prompt asks for nested dict but LLM sometimes returns flat list. Fix applied: isinstance(requirements, dict) guard with fallback to empty dict.
